text: The old bookstore had been closed for three years when Marcus discovered the handwritten note tucked behind the loose brick in the alley wall. It was dated 1987, written in careful cursive on cream-colored paper that had yellowed to the color of old teeth, and it contained nothing but a question: "If you're reading this, have you learned to forgive yourself yet?" He stood in the gathering dusk, rain beginning to fall in fat drops that darkened the pavement, and felt something shift in his chest—a recognition of words meant for him across a span of nearly four decades, though he had no idea who had written them or how they could possibly know what he'd been carrying all these years.
